mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive when the policy rate is trending down versus three months earlier.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of observations when FEDFUNDS is declining for 3 consecutive months, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays elevated, real policy tightens and valuation multiples compress; this should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days immediately after the inflation-rate crossing.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 3.5% while FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered events are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal loses sign consistency after the inflation threshold is crossed.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE signals weakening income growth and future earnings risk, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are negative when unemployment increases from its prior monthly reading.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are positive in most months with higher UNRATE than the prior month, or if the rolling 3-year correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp PAYEMS gain paired with a falling UNRATE confirms broadening labor income and consumer resilience, which supports equities over the next 20 trading days; the signal fails when payroll growth is weak or unemployment does not improve.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises by more than 150 thousand in a month and UNRATE falls or stays flat versus the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not outperform unconditional 20-day returns by at least 1 percentage point on average, or if the signal turns negative when payroll gains exceed the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger nominal retail demand and near-term revenue momentum for cyclicals, so S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days are positive when sales accelerate month over month.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward returns are not positive more than 50% of the time after month-over-month RSAFS increases, or if the rank signal has IC below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST indicates housing demand deterioration and tighter collateral conditions, which usually foreshadows weaker risk appetite and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive after the housing-drawdown trigger in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal fails to remain negative in downturn episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO improves while CPIAUCSL does not accelerate, firms are expanding output without renewed inflation pressure, supporting margins and equity multiples; this predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if months with rising INDPRO and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL do not produce above-baseline 20-day returns, or if the interaction term becomes insignificant in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, a rebound in INDPRO after prior weakness marks the start of manufacturing normalization and inventory rebuilding, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months after at least one month of decline
failure_condition: Reject if triggered rebounds fail to outperform unconditional 60-day returns by at least 1 percentage point on average, or if forward returns are not positive in a majority of recovery triggers.
